title: Log Ignored Metrics for AI Training Feedback
description:
Ignored metrics from the Metrics Analyst should be logged for future AI training to help the service learn which suggestions are not valid metrics. Two new models were created:

- `MetricApiResponse` (1:1 with user/team) stores the full first and second API response strings.
- `ApiResponseMetric` (1:many with MetricApiResponse) stores individual metrics from the response with fields for topic, metric name, description, dashboard ID, reports, classification, and an `ignore` flag.

When a user ignores a metric, the `ignore` column is updated. The original metric record is preserved. Both models use the `usedByTeam` trait for multi-tenant security.
acceptance_criteria: Ignored metrics are persisted with an ignore flag rather than being deleted
Full API response strings are stored for both Service 1 and Service 2
Individual metric records are stored with all relevant fields from the API response
Multi-tenant isolation is enforced via usedByTeam trait on both models
Ignored metrics can be queried for future AI training purposes
middleware_changes: New logic to persist API responses and update the ignore flag when a user ignores a metric recommendation.
data_model_changes: Two new models: MetricApiResponse (stores full API response strings, 1:1 with team) and ApiResponseMetric (stores individual metrics from response with ignore flag, 1:many with MetricApiResponse).
implementation_notes: Both new models use the usedByTeam trait ensuring multi-tenant data isolation. The ApiResponseMetric model has fields: team_id, Topic, metric_name, metric_description_llm, dashboard_id, reports, classification, api_responses_id, ignore.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed ignored metrics are already being logged. Two new models were created with multi-tenant security traits. Metrics are kept in the database with an ignore flag rather than being deleted.
